articles.headline: The 6 Military Aircraft With The Longest Service Lives
articles.blurb: A exploration of the world's most durable military planes.
articles.takeaway: Extended aircraft service lives indicate that robust engineering and modular design enable decades of operational utility, informing procurement strategies for cost-effective fleet modernization.

articles.headline: NASA’s X-59 returns to flight after false cockpit warning
articles.blurb: NASA’s X-59 quiet supersonic demonstrator returned to flight after investigators determined that a cockpit warning light that cut…...
articles.takeaway: False instrument warnings during critical test phases underscore the importance of robust avionics verification before experimental flight operations resume.

articles.headline: Artemis 2’s (nearly) 10-day flight around the moon
articles.blurb: The Artemis 2 mission will send humans to the vicinity of the moon for the first time in more than 50 years on a mission lasting almost 10 days.
articles.takeaway: Artemis 2's extended lunar proximity mission demonstrates NASA's commitment to sustainable human spaceflight architecture before attempting surface landings.

articles.headline: Only 52% Full: United Airlines' 10 Emptiest Long-Haul Routes Revealed

articles.takeaway: United's lowest-performing long-haul routes operate at half capacity, signaling potential network optimization opportunities and revenue management challenges on specific international markets.

articles.headline: DroneDeploy brings AI-powered site visibility to Irish housing projects
articles.blurb: If there’s one thing construction teams everywhere can agree on, it’s this: visibility is everything. And now, DroneDeploy is doubling down on that idea through a new partnership with Cairn Homes — Ireland’s leading homebuilder — to bring advanced drone reality capture tech across more than 25...
articles.takeaway: AI-powered drone analytics are enabling construction firms to optimize project timelines and resource allocation through real-time aerial site monitoring.

articles.headline: Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk
articles.blurb: Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).
articles.takeaway: Effective SRM requires organizations to systematically document why residual risks remain acceptable after implementing feasible controls, creating accountability for risk acceptance decisions.

articles.headline: Saltzman: Space ‘baked into’ modern combat operations
articles.blurb: Space Force personnel supporting operations under U.S. Central Command are positioned in the Middle East, at headquarters in Tampa, Florida, and at Shaw Air Force Base in South Carolina...
articles.takeaway: Military aviation operations now fundamentally depend on space-based systems for command, control, and intelligence, making space capabilities as critical as aircraft to modern warfare.

articles.headline: FCC eyes sweeping reforms to boost US drone power
articles.blurb: The Federal Communications Commission is making its next big move in the race to dominate drone technology, and it’s tying that push directly to President Donald Trump’s national strategy for what officials are calling “American drone dominance.” more…...
articles.takeaway: FCC regulatory reforms aim to unlock spectrum and communications infrastructure needed for US drone industry competitiveness against China and other global competitors.

articles.headline: FAA Restricts SFO Landings
articles.blurb: New FAA landing restrictions at San Francisco International Airport will cut arrival capacity by one-third.
articles.takeaway: SFO's one-third capacity reduction will force major airlines to consolidate schedules and potentially increase connecting traffic through less-congested hubs, reshaping West Coast route networks.

articles.headline: The Evolution of Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.
articles.takeaway: Modern SMS shifts accountability from post-incident investigation to continuous hazard identification, fundamentally changing how airlines and operators prevent accidents before they occur.

articles.headline: Aspect Aerospace secures early funding to advance swarm-deployable VLEO satellites
articles.blurb: Aspect Aerospace, a University of South Alabama spin-off, has secured $2.4 million to develop circuit-board-sized spacecraft that could be deployed from space into very low Earth orbit in swarms to monitor the space environment.
articles.takeaway: Circuit-board-sized satellites deployable in swarms from space could revolutionize persistent Earth observation and space situational awareness at lower costs than traditional orbital systems.

articles.headline: F-35 Crashes in Nevada, Pilot Ejected Safely
articles.blurb: An F-35 went down 25 miles northeast of Indian Springs, inside the NTTR. According to the U.S. Air Force, the pilot ejected safely and was treated for minor injuries. A U.S. Air Force F-35A Lightning II fighter jet crashed during a mission over the Nevada Test & Training Range (NTTR) on Mar. 31,...
articles.takeaway: F-35 ejection seat performance remains effective under emergency conditions, though loss-of-aircraft incidents underscore ongoing reliability challenges in fifth-generation fighter operations.

articles.headline: Hawaii Air Tour Operator Suspends Flights After Fatal Crash
articles.blurb: Airborne Aviation has suspended operations indefinitely following last week's fatal helicopter crash.
articles.takeaway: Operator self-suspension signals industry recognition that regulatory and safety protocol gaps may require immediate corrective actions beyond accident investigation.

articles.headline: Pentagon signs seven-year deal with Boeing to triple PAC-3 seeker output
articles.blurb: The Pentagon has signed a seven-year framework agreement with Boeing to triple the production of seekers for the…...
articles.takeaway: Boeing's PAC-3 seeker production tripling signals sustained defense contractor reliance on advanced missile guidance technology through 2031, directly impacting aerospace manufacturing capacity allocation.

articles.headline: Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)
articles.blurb: Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...
articles.takeaway: Wire strike fatalities persist despite pilot overconfidence in reaction time, making situational awareness and route planning critical defensive measures for rotorcraft operators.

articles.headline: LATAM becomes first South American carrier to roll out Wi-Fi on long-haul routes
articles.blurb: LATAM Airlines has completed its first long-haul flight equipped with internet connectivity. The new service was introduced on…...
articles.takeaway: LATAM's Wi-Fi deployment on long-haul routes signals competitive pressure from North American carriers and positions South American airlines to retain premium passengers on intercontinental flights.

articles.headline: Philippine Airlines profits rise to $160 million in 2025 despite higher costs
articles.blurb: Philippine Airlines (PAL) closed 2025 with a net income of $160.4 million, a 6.1% increase from the previous…...
articles.takeaway: PAL's profitability growth outpaced cost inflation, suggesting successful revenue management and operational efficiency improvements in a competitive Southeast Asian market.

articles.headline: Creating near-term lunar settlements: lessons from space history
articles.blurb: March 16, 2026, was the 100th anniversary of Robert Goddard’s first flight of a liquid fueled rocket. It reached an altitude of 41 feet. 31 years later, in 1957, Sputnik began a lonely beep as the first satellite in orbit. In 1969, 12 years after Sputnik and 43 years after Goddard’s first flight,...
articles.takeaway: Historical precedent suggests lunar settlement infrastructure requires phased development timelines spanning decades, not years, based on the progression from suborbital flight to orbital capability to crewed missions.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational efficiency gains across airlines, airports, and flight systems can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental concerns without requiring wholesale fleet replacement.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: WB-57 and Secretive RAT-55 Spotted Rehearsing for NASA’s Artemis Launch
articles.blurb: The secretive U.S. Air Force NT-43A RAT55 was spotted flying with a NASA callsign in one of the areas reserved for the launch of the Artemis II mission, opposite to the one used by the WB-57 Canberra. NASA is set to launch the Artemis II mission on Apr. 1, 2026, at 6:24 pm EDT (22:24 […]...
articles.takeaway: NASA's Artemis II lunar mission will employ specialized high-altitude aircraft including the classified RAT-55 for atmospheric monitoring and data collection during launch operations.

articles.headline: Artemis 2 fueling underway
articles.blurb: Fueling of the SLS is underway for an April 1 launch attempt of Artemis 2, the first mission to send humans toward the moon in more than 50 years.
articles.takeaway: Artemis 2's fueling progression signals NASA's readiness to conduct the most complex crewed lunar mission architecture since Apollo, requiring aerospace supply chain coordination across multiple propellant systems.

articles.headline: Boeing's Higher MTOW 787 Dreamliners Cleared By FAA
articles.blurb: While we’ve already covered some of this news through our March 9th video, it was only on March 23rd that the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) certified Boeing’s increased maximum takeoff weights (iMTOWs) for the 787-9 and 787-10.
articles.takeaway: FAA certification of increased 787 MTOW enables operators to carry additional payload or fuel, directly improving route economics and operational flexibility for long-haul carriers.

articles.headline: Why the 787-10 uses fewer wheels than the A350-1000
articles.blurb: Why In The World Does The Boeing 787-10 Dreamliner Have Fewer Wheels Than The Airbus A350-1000?At first glance, the landing gear of modern widebody aircraft can look almost identical. Large twin-engine jets sit low on the ground, supported by multiple wheels arranged in similar patterns, and unless...
articles.takeaway: The 787-10's lighter composite airframe enables reduced landing gear complexity compared to heavier aluminum aircraft like the A350-1000, demonstrating material science advantages in reducing maintenance costs and structural weight.

articles.headline: Why Do Cargo Carriers Still Avoid Twin-Engine Replacements?
articles.blurb: Older jets can still be highly valuable if they offer the right mix of range, volume, and operating economics.
articles.takeaway: Legacy cargo aircraft remain economically competitive because operators can optimize routes and payload configurations that newer twin-engine designs haven't fully displaced on profitability metrics.

articles.headline: After three years, Artemis 2 astronauts ready to launch
articles.blurb: Even after three years of public appearances as a crew, there can still be some surprises for the Artemis 2 astronauts.
articles.takeaway: Extended pre-flight timelines for crewed lunar missions create sustained public engagement that can reveal unexpected crew dynamics despite years of joint preparation.

articles.takeaway: The 777X's extended range could enable airlines to operate direct ultra-long-haul routes that bypass traditional hub-and-spoke networks, potentially reshaping global aviation connectivity.
